heading: Container security standards and procedures
label: 944 Container security standards and procedures
notes: Amendments2007—Subsec. (a)(4). , which directed amendment of par. (4) by substituting “(1) Deadline for enforcement” and subpar. (A) designation and heading for “(1) Deadline for enforcement”, was executed by inserting the subpar. (a)(4)(B). , added subpar. (B).Pub. L. 110–53, § 1701(b)(2)

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Secretary shall initiate a rulemaking proceeding to establish minimum standards and procedures for securing containers in transit to the United States.October 13, 2006

children.heading: Interim rule
children.label: 2 Interim rule
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall issue an interim final rule pursuant to the proceeding described in paragraph (1).October 13, 2006

children.heading: Missed deadline
children.label: 3 Missed deadline
children.text: If the Secretary is unable to meet the deadline established pursuant to paragraph (2), the Secretary shall submit a letter to the appropriate congressional committees explaining why the Secretary is unable to meet that deadline and describing what must be done before such minimum standards and procedures can be established.

children.heading: Enforcement of rule
children.label: A Enforcement of rule
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which the standards and procedures are established pursuant to paragraph (1), all containers bound for ports of entry in the United States shall meet such standards and procedures.

children.label: i
children.text: effective not later than , all containers in transit to the United States shall be required to meet the requirements of International Organization for Standardization Publicly Available Specification 17712 standard for sealing containers; andOctober 15, 2008

children.label: ii
children.text: the requirements of this subparagraph shall cease to be effective upon the effective date of the interim final rule issued pursuant to this subsection.

children.heading: Review and enhancement
children.label: b Review and enhancement
children.text: The Secretary shall regularly review and enhance the standards and procedures established pursuant to subsection (a), as appropriate, based on tests of technologies as they become commercially available to detect container intrusion and the highest consequence threats, particularly weapons of mass destruction.

children.heading: International cargo security standards
children.label: c International cargo security standards
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of State, the Secretary of Energy, and other Federal Government officials, as appropriate, and with the Commercial Operations Advisory Committee, the Homeland Security Advisory Committee, and the National Maritime Security Advisory Committee, is encouraged to promote and establish international standards for the security of containers moving through the international supply chain with foreign governments and international organizations, including the International Maritime Organization, the International Organization for Standardization, the International Labor Organization, and the World Customs Organization.

children.heading: International trade and other obligations
children.label: d International trade and other obligations
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall consult with appropriate Federal departments and agencies and private sector stakeholders and ensure that actions under this section do not violate international trade obligations or other international obligations of the United States.
